perturbation.original_text: Allied shall comply with: (a) all applicable laws, rules, regulations, regulatory policies, guidelines or codes applicable to the Arena and Allied's activities to be carried out in performing its obligations in accordance with this Agreement, including all such guidelines and codes issued by statutory, regulatory and industry bodies, and further, will not pay, deliver, or offer or promise to pay or deliver, any funds or other item of value excluding the Products, either directly or through any third party, to any state or federal governmental official for any reason whatsoever other than the payment of statutory and administrative fees, charges and taxes that are due from Allied as a result of its performance under this Agreement; (b) the terms and conditions, rules of conduct and/or community guidelines of any other online platform (including any advertising policies); and (c) any conditions attached to any licences or consents issued in connection with the Arena including regarding health and safety and crowd security measures at the Arena.
perturbation.changed_text: Allied shall endeavor to comply with: (a) all applicable laws, rules, regulations, regulatory policies, guidelines or codes applicable to the Arena and Allied's activities to be carried out in performing its obligations in accordance with this Agreement, including all such guidelines and codes issued by statutory, regulatory and industry bodies, and will refrain from paying, delivering, or offering or promising to pay or deliver, any funds or other item of value excluding the Products, either directly or through any third party, to any state or federal governmental official for any reason whatsoever other than the payment of statutory and administrative fees, charges and taxes that are due from Allied as a result of its performance under this Agreement; (b) the terms and conditions, rules of conduct and/or community guidelines of any other online platform (including any advertising policies); and (c) any conditions attached to any licences or consents issued in connection with the Arena including regarding health and safety and crowd security measures at the Arena.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Allied shall comply' to 'Allied shall endeavor to comply,' the obligation becomes ambiguous and less stringent. The term 'endeavor' suggests an effort rather than a guarantee of compliance, which is a lower standard. Additionally, changing "will not pay, deliver, or offer or promise" to "will refrain from paying, delivering, or offering or promising" introduces ambiguity, as 'refrain' is less forceful than a direct prohibition.
perturbation.law_explanation: The modification weakens the commitment to prevent bribery of foreign officials. The FCPA requires strict adherence to anti-bribery provisions. By stating Allied will only 'endeavor' to comply and 'refrain' from bribery, it suggests that bribery may be acceptable if efforts to prevent it fail, which directly contradicts the FCPA's prohibition on bribery. The original clause unambiguously prohibited bribery, while the modified clause creates an opening for interpretation that could allow for corrupt practices under the guise of 'endeavoring' to comply.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Criminal Division | Foreign Corrupt Practices Act Unit Skip to main content MENU Foreign Corrupt Practices Act Unit Corporate Enforcement Policy Statutes Legislative History FCPA Guidelines FCPA Resource Guide Enforcement Actions A - M N - Z Chronological List Corporate Enforcement Actions Individual Enforcement Actions FCPA Opinions Opinion Procedure Regulations (PDF) Opinion Releases Index Summaries Opinion Procedure Releases Review Procedure Releases International Agreements Anti-Corruption Sites Foreign Corrupt Practices Act Unit Share Facebook X LinkedIn Email An Overview Foreign Corrupt Practices Act The Foreign Corrupt Practices Act of 1977, as amended, 15 U.S.C. §§ 78dd-1, et seq. ("FCPA"), was enacted for the purpose of making it unlawful for certain classes of persons and entities to make payments to foreign government officials to assist in obtaining or retaining business. Specifically, the anti-bribery provisions of the FCPA prohibit the willful use of the mails or any means of instrumentality of interstate commerce corruptly in furtherance of any offer, payment, promise to pay, or authorization of the payment of money or anything of value to any person, while knowing that all or a portion of such money or thing of value will be offered, given or promised, directly or indirectly, to a foreign official to influence the foreign official in his or her official capacity, induce the foreign official to do or omit to do an act in violation of his or her lawful duty, or to secure any improper advantage in order to assist in obtaining or retaining business for or with, or directing business to, any person. Since 1977, the anti-bribery provisions of the FCPA have applied to all U.S. persons and certain foreign issuers of securities. With the enactment of certain amendments in 1998, the anti-bribery provisions of the FCPA now also apply to foreign firms and persons who cause, directly or through agents, an act in furtherance of such a corrupt payment to take place within the territory of the United States. The FCPA also requires companies whose securities are listed in the United States to meet its accounting provisions. See 15 U.S.C. § 78m. These accounting provisions, which were designed to operate in tandem with the anti-bribery provisions of the FCPA, require corporations covered by the provisions to (a) make and keep books and records that accurately and fairly reflect the transactions of the corporation and (b) devise and maintain an adequate system of internal accounting controls. For particular FCPA compliance questions relating to specific conduct, you should seek the advice of counsel as well as consider using t
perturbation.justification: The modified contract introduces ambiguity regarding compliance with anti-bribery provisions, potentially contradicting the Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA). By changing "Allied shall comply" to "Allied shall endeavor to comply," the obligation becomes less stringent and allows for interpretation where full compliance may not be guaranteed. Similarly, changing "will not pay, deliver, or offer or promise" to "will refrain from paying, delivering, or offering or promising" weakens the prohibition against bribery. Scraped Law Snippet 2 states that the FCPA prohibits the "willful use of the mails or any means of instrumentality of interstate commerce corruptly in furtherance of any offer, payment, promise to pay..." The modified language, suggesting Allied will only 'endeavor' to comply and 'refrain' from bribery, introduces a loophole potentially allowing for corrupt practices under the guise of attempted compliance. This directly undermines the FCPA's intent to strictly prohibit bribery of foreign officials.

perturbation.original_text: Allied confirms that, whenever possible, it will ensure that Newegg Marks will be present in accordance with this Agreement and that Newegg Marks are incorporated into all promotional, advertising and publicity material published in connection with the Arena where reasonably practicable.
perturbation.changed_text: Allied will aim to include Newegg Marks in promotional, advertising, and publicity material, subject to its overall marketing strategy.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates the inclusion of Newegg Marks 'whenever possible' and 'where reasonably practicable.' The modified text uses the phrase 'aim to include' and 'subject to its overall marketing strategy,' making the inclusion of Newegg Marks discretionary and dependent on Allied's marketing decisions. This weakens the original obligation and introduces ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_law: California Business and Professions Code Section 14320
perturbation.law_explanation: This modification could violate California Business and Professions Code Section 14320, which addresses trademark dilution. If the Newegg Marks are not prominently and consistently displayed as per the original agreement, the ambiguity introduced could result in the weakening or tarnishment of the Newegg brand in the eyes of consumers. The new wording allows Allied to prioritize its own marketing strategy over the agreed-upon promotion of Newegg Marks, potentially harming Newegg's brand and diluting its trademark.

perturbation.justification: The original contract mandates Newegg Marks be present 'whenever possible' and 'where reasonably practicable.' The changed text uses 'aim to include' and 'subject to its overall marketing strategy.' This makes the inclusion of Newegg Marks discretionary, weakening the original obligation and introducing ambiguity. This ambiguity, as suggested by the Law Explanation, could violate California Business and Professions Code Section 14320 regarding trademark dilution if the Newegg Marks are not consistently displayed, and the change allows Allied to prioritize its own marketing strategy, potentially harming Newegg's brand. Scraped Law Snippet 1 shows the ability to search for this California Code (BPC 14320) which is the law allegedly violated. This introduces a significant legal risk because the contract's altered language allows for non-compliance with potential trademark obligations, making enforcement problematic and therefore creating a legal contradiction as defined in the instructions.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties shall co-operate in good faith on all announcements and press releases regarding this Agreement and Newegg's sponsorship arrangement with Allied and Newegg shall determine in its sole discretion whether any such announcement or press release shall be published. Press releases concerning Newegg's sponsorship of the Arena will only be published after written preapproval by both Parties and Newegg shall have the final decision making right concerning any press releases regarding Newegg's sponsorship arrangement with Allied.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties will generally cooperate on announcements and press releases, but Newegg will typically decide whether a release is published. Press releases concerning Newegg's sponsorship will usually require written preapproval, but Newegg has the right to publish such releases.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'shall cooperate in good faith' with 'will generally cooperate' introduces ambiguity and lowers the standard of cooperation. Replacing 'Newegg shall determine in its sole discretion' with 'Newegg will typically decide' softens the obligation. Changing 'will only be published after written preapproval by both Parties' to 'will usually require written preapproval' makes the preapproval process optional and less enforceable.
perturbation.law_explanation: This modification contradicts California Commercial Code Section 1203, which implies an obligation of good faith in the performance or enforcement of every contract or duty within the code. By weakening the language from 'shall cooperate in good faith' to 'will generally cooperate' and making preapproval optional, the clause creates room for one party (Newegg) to act in a manner that could be considered bad faith, such as unilaterally issuing press releases that negatively impact Allied, thereby violating the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing.

GENERAL PROVISIONS [1101 - 1310] ( Division 1 enacted by Stats. 1963, Ch. 819. ) CHAPTER 2. General Definitions and Principles of Interpretation [1201 - 1206] ( Chapter 2 enacted by Stats. 1963, Ch. 819. ) 1203. (a) Whether a transaction in the form of a lease creates a lease or security interest is determined by the facts of each case. (b) A transaction in the form of a lease creates a security interest if the consideration that the lessee is to pay the lessor for the right to possession and use of the goods is an obligation for the term of the lease and is not subject to termination by the lessee, and: (1) the original term of the lease is equal to or greater than the remaining economic life of the goods; (2) the lessee is bound to renew the lease for the remaining economic life of the goods or is bound to become the owner of the goods; (3) the lessee has an option to renew the lease for the remaining economic life of the goods for no additional consideration or for nominal additional consideration upon compliance with the lease agreement; or (4) the lessee has an option to become the owner of the goods for no additional consideration or for nominal additional consideration upon compliance with the lease agreement. (c) A transact
perturbation.justification: The modification weakens the original contract's obligation to cooperate in good faith, potentially contradicting the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing, as the Law Explanation suggests. California Commercial Code Section 1203, provided in Scraped Law Snippet 1, implies an obligation of good faith in the performance or enforcement of every contract. The changed text introduces ambiguity regarding the level of cooperation and the requirement for preapproval, which can be interpreted as a departure from the good faith requirement outlined in the law. The change from 'shall cooperate in good faith' to 'will generally cooperate' and making preapproval optional introduces ambiguity and lowers the standard of cooperation.
